name: Brute Force
description: Adversaries may use brute force techniques to gain access to accounts by systematically guessing passwords, often generating many failed authentication attempts in a short window.

name: Password Guessing
description: Adversaries may guess passwords for a known list of usernames without prior knowledge of policy or specific passwords, often against a single or small set of accounts.

name: Valid Accounts
description: Adversaries may obtain and abuse credentials of existing accounts to gain initial access, persistence, or privilege escalation, which can be difficult to detect because the activity uses legitimate accounts.

name: Valid Accounts: Local Accounts
description: Adversaries may obtain and abuse credentials of a local account, such as a built-in root or administrator account, to gain elevated access to a system.

name: Remote Services: SSH
description: Adversaries may use SSH to move laterally or establish remote access within a network, often reusing legitimate credentials over the SSH protocol.

name: Valid Accounts: Cloud Accounts
description: Logins occurring at unusual times or from unexpected locations relative to an account's normal behavior baseline can indicate use of compromised or shared credentials.
